title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node process, event loop, modules, configuration, and lifecycle and demands the smallest safe process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Event Loop Work Queues · changed case · Quiz Event Loop Work Queues
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 3951 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; reject a replay before any external effect; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
